defect.statement: registry/verdicts/f2.json field scale_language_licensed = "slope" is over-licensed. MEASURED: tools/registry/verdict-gen.py (pre-fix line 560) computed the license from the raw distinct-rung-label count only; f2's three rungs {R1, R2, R3} mapped to "slope" with no check against the frozen envelope or the realized trend result.
defect.class: verdict-generator code defect (claim-governance metadata over-license); NOT the same failure mode as a5-llm: MEASURED, f2's three rungs ARE comparable model-scale rungs (SmolLM2 135M/360M/1.7B pinned revisions — no baseline label in rungs_measured), so the G-12 count map lawfully reached slope-ELIGIBLE. The over-license is against the OTHER two per-record caps: (1) the frozen HE1 envelope caps trend language at direction-only; (2) no valid section-2 scale-trend fit exists in the analysis output for a 'slope' license to describe.
evidence: MEASURED: registry/verdicts/f2.json (sha256 38422b3caf5df8a339d723eaa198a26f783465667fb920c34dbb697071dd6b2e) extrapolation_envelope_verbatim row HE1: 'Measured range: (135M,360M),(360M,1.7B). Envelope: 3 rungs => gap-closure-fraction trend to 7B, direction-only; bias stated: the s->S gap itself narrows as S grows'

MEASURED: reports/auto/f2/analysis-output.json (sha256 5952a7ff26e439c6dcd99f18305becbc0a7e2dc70a91dc8041bd909316f567e2) contains NO section-2 WLS trend fit — only gap_closed_fraction_R1R2 and gap_closed_fraction_R2R3 point values — so no licensed slope fit was ever produced

MEASURED: registry/verdicts/f2.json verdict = FAIL (fired rule index 1); no positive scale claim is carried by this record and no downstream document leans on its license field (docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md section 2.4 sweep: blast radius of the generator defect in issued records is exactly a5-llm and f2; no CONFIRMED verdict carries an over-license)

binding_reading.ruling: DECISION: the binding reading of f2's scale_language_licensed is 'sign-only' (the generator's established vocabulary; in the frozen HE1 envelope's own words the licensed trend language is DIRECTION-ONLY — a gap-closure-fraction direction toward 7B, never a slope law). Downstream documents must not state any slope or scaling-law claim from f2. Low-stakes hygiene: the verdict is FAIL and nothing leans on this record's license.
binding_reading.no_measured_number_affected: MEASURED: no endpoint value, fired rule, or verdict changes; the license field is claim-governance metadata only.

corrective_action.record_mutation: NONE. registry/experiments/f2.json, results-log/f2.jsonl, registry/verdicts/f2.json and registry/frozen-index.json stand byte-identical as issued (append-never-edit). This erratum record is the binding correction of the license field until any lawful regeneration under the maintainer's decision-1b ruling.
corrective_action.generator_fix: tools/registry/verdict-gen.py is fixed in the same change-set (per-record min-rule; see registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json for the fix description). Regenerating the f2 verdict with the fixed generator yields sign-only (no machine-readable design.scale_language_max ceiling and no valid trend result => slope is refused, fail closed).
corrective_action.verdict_impact: registry/verdicts/f2.json FAIL stands unchanged; this erratum narrows claim-governance metadata only.
